reasoning: ASTS down 10.4% today on SpaceX IPO news creating a potential misread of competitive dynamics. The market is treating SpaceX's Starlink and AST SpaceMobile as direct competitors when they serve fundamentally different use cases (satellite internet vs. direct-to-cell). This selloff on unrelated news creates an analytical opportunity to frame the actual competitive moat, though not enough conviction yet for full initiation given pre-revenue status and execution risk.
title: AST SpaceMobile: Why the SpaceX IPO Selloff Misses the Point
angle: Market conflating Starlink broadband with AST's direct-to-existing-handset technology; explaining why carrier partnerships (AT&T, Verizon agreements) create a different TAM entirely and why SpaceX going public might actually validate the space-connectivity theme rather than threaten ASTS specifically.
